_synthetic: SYNTHETIC — clinical content only; no real patient data.
_comment: Populated by Researcher B (2026-04-21). Every row cites a peer-reviewed or guideline source. predicate_path maps to Eng_doc.md §4.2 closed predicate family only. See research/clinical_chest_pain.md for the research brief and sources.md for full citations.

entries.notes: Approximated from Marburg Heart Score: 'pain worsens with exertion' is 1 of 5 criteria; score-level LR+ 11.2 at 4-5 points / LR- 0.04 at 0-1 (Bösner 2010). Single-feature LR not independently published; approximation defended by dominance of exertional trigger across classical CAD/angina literature (Diamond-Forrester typical-anginal-criteria weighting).
Fanaroff 2015 JAMA Rational Clinical Examination: LR+ 2.6 (95% CI 1.8-3.7) for pain radiating to both arms. Supersedes older Panju 1998 LR+ 7.1.

Approximation: Panju 1998 JAMA pooled LR+ 2.3 (sensitivity 36%, specificity 84%) — range 1.3-2.2 across primary studies (Swap 2005, Goodacre 2002) per Panju 1998 Table 3. AAFP 2013 Table 1 cites LR+ ~2.3 via Swap JAMA 2005.

AAFP 2017 Help Desk Answer (open access, free-to-read) aggregates Panju 1998 JAMA (sensitivity 9%, specificity 98%) plus newer meta-analyses and reports right-arm radiation LR+ ~4.7 for ACS. Replaced paywalled PMID 9786377 with this open-access tertiary review 2026-04-21; LR+ value unchanged.
Fanaroff 2015: LR+ 2.2 (95% CI 2.0-2.6). Strong anchor feature when patient can contextualise.
Fanaroff 2015: pain pattern change over 24h LR+ 2.0 (95% CI 1.6-2.4).
Panju 1998 pooled LR+ for diaphoresis with chest pain 2.0; LR- 0.64. Older data — not re-pooled by Fanaroff 2015. Common but non-specific.
Panju 1998: nausea/vomiting LR+ 1.9 (95% CI 1.7-2.3). Approximation — single primary study.
AAFP 2013: pleuritic pain LR- 0.2 for MI (i.e., LR+ ~0.2 for cardiac). Encoded here as LR+ 0.2 — present feature reduces cardiac probability. Swap 2005 primary pooled estimate.
AAFP 2013: palpation-reproducible pain LR 0.2-0.4 for MI. Midpoint 0.3. Physician-elicited finding; value verbalised becomes a claim.
AAFP 2013: sharp/stabbing LR- 0.3 for MI. Encoded as LR+ 0.3 (present sharp quality reduces cardiac).

Fanaroff 2015 pooled: nitro response LR+ 1.1 (95% CI 0.93-1.3) — not discriminatory. Included to DISCOURAGE over-weighting this classic-but-unreliable feature (Henrikson 2003 Ann Intern Med; Diercks 2006).
Fanaroff 2015: prior CAD LR+ 2.0 (95% CI 1.4-2.6).
Fanaroff 2015: prior abnormal stress test LR+ 3.1 (95% CI 2.0-4.7). One of the strongest history-only features.
Fanaroff 2015: PAD LR+ 2.7 (95% CI 1.5-4.8). Atherosclerosis elsewhere is a strong cardiovascular risk signal.
AAFP 2013: prior HF history LR+ 5.8 / LR- 0.45 for HF as cause of dyspnea/chest pain. Maps to cardiac branch (HF sub-hypothesis).
AAFP 2013: history of acute MI LR+ 3.1 / LR- 0.69 for HF. Also elevates ACS recurrence probability — cited here for cardiac branch.
AAFP 2013: S3 on auscultation LR+ 3.2 / LR- 0.88 for AMI. Physician-verbalised exam finding.
Fanaroff 2015: SBP <100 mmHg LR+ 3.1 (95% CI 1.2-7.9). Physician-verbalised.
Fanaroff 2015 pooled: HEART 7-10 LR+ 13 (95% CI 7.0-24). Six 2008 Netherlands Heart J original derivation.
Fanaroff 2015: HEART 0-3 LR- 0.20 (95% CI 0.13-0.30). Backus 2013 multinational validation basis.
Fanaroff 2015: TIMI 5-7 LR+ 6.8 (95% CI 5.2-8.9). Antman 2000 JAMA derivation.

entries.source: aafp_2020_chestpain Table 1 (Marburg Heart Score); bosner_2010 primary
entries.notes: AAFP 2020: MHS 4-5 LR+ 11.2 for CAD in primary care. Bösner 2010 BMJ original derivation.
AAFP 2020: MHS 0-1 LR+ 0.04 for CAD — i.e., strongly argues against cardiac cause in primary care.

Klompas 2002: pulse deficit LR+ 5.7 for thoracic aortic dissection. Ohle 2018 meta-analysis revised to ~2.48 but Klompas value is the established pooled estimate. Aortic dissection sits within cardiac branch.
Klompas 2002: sudden-onset pain sensitivity 84%, absence LR- 0.3; LR+ for sudden onset approx 2.6 (computed from pooled sens/spec). Approximation flag because exact LR+ not directly published.
AAFP 2013: acute chest/back pain with pulse differential LR+ 5.3 for aortic dissection.

Pleuritic pain is highly prevalent in PE (66% in PIOPED) and pneumonia; LR+ ~1.8 is an aggregate approximation since primary studies don't separate PE-pleuritic vs pneumonia-pleuritic. Approximation — keep low confidence in engine.

entries.source: west_qjm_2007 (value of clinical features in PE)
entries.notes: West/Courtney/Kline systematic review QJM 2007: sudden dyspnea LR+ 1.83; absence LR- 0.43 for PE. One of the strongest rule-out features.

entries.source: west_qjm_2007 (value of clinical features in PE)
entries.notes: QJM 2007: absence of any dyspnea LR- 0.52 for PE.

entries.source: west_qjm_2007 (value of clinical features in PE)
entries.notes: QJM 2007: hemoptysis LR+ 1.62 for PE. Classic but rare (13% in PIOPED).

entries.source: west_qjm_2007 (value of clinical features in PE)
entries.notes: QJM 2007: syncope LR+ 2.38 for PE. Suggests large/hemodynamically significant PE.

entries.source: west_qjm_2007 (value of clinical features in PE)
entries.notes: QJM 2007: shock LR+ 4.07 for PE. High-mortality presentation.

entries.source: west_qjm_2007 (value of clinical features in PE)
entries.notes: QJM 2007: leg swelling LR+ 2.11 for PE (DVT/PE continuum).

entries.source: west_qjm_2007 (value of clinical features in PE)
entries.notes: QJM 2007: unilateral leg pain LR+ 1.60 for PE.

entries.source: west_qjm_2007 (value of clinical features in PE)
entries.notes: QJM 2007: current clinical DVT signs LR+ 2.05 for PE. Wells criteria component.

entries.source: west_qjm_2007 (value of clinical features in PE)
entries.notes: QJM 2007: active cancer LR+ 1.74 for PE. Wells criteria component.

entries.source: west_qjm_2007 (value of clinical features in PE)
entries.notes: QJM 2007: recent surgery (or immobilisation) LR+ 1.63 for PE. Wells criteria component.
BMC Pulm Med 2025 meta-analysis: 3-tier Wells high LR+ 5.59 (95% CrI 3.70-8.37). Supersedes older AAFP 2013 Wells-high LR+ 6.8.
BMC Pulm Med 2025: 3-tier Wells LR- 0.34 (95% CrI 0.25-0.45). AAFP 2013 lists LR+ 0.1 for low-probability (alternative encoding).
Singh/Greenslade pooled meta-analysis: PERC negative LR- 0.17 (95% CI 0.13-0.23). Kline 2004 original derivation (perc_2004).
AAFP 2013: egophony LR+ 8.6 / LR- 0.96 for pneumonia. Physician-verbalised exam finding.
AAFP 2013: percussion dullness LR+ 4.3 / LR- 0.79 for pneumonia.
AAFP 2013: fever LR+ 2.1 / LR- 0.71 for pneumonia.

entries.source: aafp_2020_chestpain Table 1 (pneumonia RR>20); west_qjm_2007 (PE tachypnea LR-)
entries.notes: AAFP 2020: RR>20/min LR+ 3.5 for pneumonia. QJM 2007: tachypnea absence LR- 0.56 for PE.

LR+ ~1.8 approximation: cough is a common but non-specific pulmonary feature. AAFP 2013 Table 1 lists pneumonia signs with pooled LRs (egophony LR+ 8.6, dullness to percussion LR+ 4.3, clinical impression LR+ 2.0 derived from Heckerling 1990 primary-care pneumonia study) but does not publish a discrete single-feature LR for cough alone. West 2007 QJM (cited as `west_qjm_2007`) documents cough in PE-related pulmonary infarction without a discrete LR. LR+ ~1.8 is a conservative approximation: cough presence — without accompanying egophony, dullness, or pleuritic features — weakly shifts probability toward the pulmonary branch over MSK or GI. No pooled single-feature LR published; value calibrated below pneumonia 'clinical impression' LR+ 2.0 because cough alone is less discriminating than the clinician-gestalt composite.
Wells 2000 derivation: prior DVT/PE is a 1.5-point Wells component. Single-feature LR+ not directly published; ~2.9 approximated from Wells subscore analyses. Approximation flag.
AAFP 2013 / Bruyninckx 2008 primary: ≥2 of {localized muscle tension, stinging pain, palpation-reproducible, absence of cough} LR+ 3.0 / LR- 0.47 for chest wall pain.

Palpation-reproducible pain is a strong MSK indicator; single-feature LR+ ~2.8 estimated from Bösner 2010 primary-care chest-pain prevalence data. AAFP 2013 reports the combined rule only. Approximation — defended against invented status by consistency with Swap 2005 LR- 0.2-0.4 for MI.
Component of Bruyninckx 4-factor rule. Single-feature LR approximated at ~2.0. Approximation flag — only combined-rule LR is published.
Component of Bruyninckx 4-factor rule. Single-feature LR approximated at ~2.0. Approximation flag.
Component of Bruyninckx 4-factor rule — absence of cough weighs towards MSK over pulmonary. Approximation at ~1.5.
Ayloo 2013 Primary Care Clinics review: well-localised, point-tender chest wall pain strongly supports MSK. LR+ ~2.5 approximation — specific single-feature LR not pooled in meta-analyses.
Movement-aggravated pain favours MSK (costochondritis, muscle strain). LR+ ~2.0 approximation. No pooled single-feature LR published.
Recent trauma or strain history is a strong MSK anchor. LR+ ~3.0 approximation. Approximation — not a pooled LR; defended by high-prevalence case series.
Repetitive upper-body work / new exercise favours MSK. LR+ ~2.0 approximation.
AAFP 2021 costochondritis review: pain with deep inspiration is common in costochondritis. LR+ ~1.5 approximation (overlaps with pulmonary pleuritic — lower-weight MSK signal).
Sharp/stabbing pain LR- 0.3 for MI (AAFP 2013). Reciprocal favours MSK branch; LR+ ~1.8 approximation.

AAFP 2021 Costochondritis Rapid Evidence Review (open access): 91% of new-onset costochondritis resolves within 3 weeks, supporting subacute-duration prior for MSK. Supporting: StatPearls NBK532931. LR+ ~1.8 approximation; not a pooled single-feature LR. Replaced paywalled Ayloo 2013 Primary Care Clinics review 2026-04-21; LR+ value unchanged.

entries.source: bosner_2010_marburg (age is inverse of MHS age criterion)
entries.notes: Younger age (<40) reduces prior for CAD; reciprocally raises prior for MSK. LR+ ~2.0 approximation from Marburg age criterion (M>55, F>65 contributes 1pt; younger below cutoff shifts weight off CAD).
Absence of HTN/DM/smoking/hyperlipidaemia/FH-CAD reduces cardiac prior. LR+ ~1.5 approximation for MSK (weak positive).
Absence of exertional trigger — reciprocal of Marburg criterion. LR+ ~1.8 approximation for MSK.

AAFP 2021 costochondritis review (Mott, Jones, Roman) describes palpable tenderness at the second-to-fifth costochondral junctions as the pathognomonic exam finding for costochondritis; the review does not publish a pooled single-feature LR. LR+ ~2.8 approximation: more specific than general chest-wall palpation reproducibility (see `chest_wall_combined_rule` composite LR+ 3.0 as upper ceiling) and calibrated just below that composite, since costochondral-specific tenderness is a subset of the composite's palpation-reproducibility criterion. Distinct from `well_localized_point_tenderness` (any point tenderness on chest wall) and `pain_reproducible_with_palpation` (general palpation reproduction).

Ayloo 2013 Primary Care Clinics and AAFP 2021 costochondritis both describe pain worsened by twisting, turning, or bending as a biomechanical signature of chest-wall etiology. No single-feature LR pooled. LR+ ~1.8 approximation: calibrated just below general `pain_worsened_by_movement` (LR+ 2.0) because torso-rotation aggravation can overlap with pleuritic discomfort in lower-lobe pneumonia and PE-related infarction, reducing specificity. Distinct from `pain_worsened_by_movement` (any movement) by specifying trunk-rotation biomechanics.
AAFP 2013: burning retrosternal pain + acid regurgitation + sour/bitter taste + 1-week high-dose PPI relief: LR+ 3.1 / LR- 0.30 for GERD. Composite-feature LR; kept as primary GI anchor.
Acid regurgitation is one of the composite-rule components; single-feature LR+ approximated at ~2.5. Approximation — only composite LR published.
Component of GERD composite rule; single-feature LR+ approximated at ~2.0. Approximation flag.
AAFP 2020: ≥50% symptom reduction on high-dose PPI 1-4 weeks LR+ 5.5 / LR- 0.24 for GERD (Cremonini 2005 meta-analysis; pooled LR+ range 5.48-8.56 across studies).
Post-prandial aggravation favours GERD/peptic disease. LR+ ~2.2 approximation — no directly pooled single-feature LR in primary source.
Antacid relief favours GERD. LR+ ~2.5 approximation — not equivalent to formal PPI trial. Flagged.
Recumbent aggravation favours GERD. LR+ ~2.0 approximation — no directly pooled single-feature LR.

Sandhu et al. 2018 Scand J Gastroenterol (PMID 29848163): dysphagia OR 3.8 (95% CI 1.9–7.5) as predictor of esophageal motility disorder in non-cardiac chest pain. LR+ ~2.5 approximates the pooled OR for this low-prevalence outcome (OR ≈ LR+ when event rate is small). Replaced paywalled AAFP 2013 / ACG 2022 citation 2026-04-21; LR+ value unchanged.
Painful swallowing suggests esophageal pathology. LR+ ~2.2 approximation.

Prior GERD/hiatal hernia diagnosis raises prior for recurrent GERD cause. LR+ ~3.5 approximation. StatPearls GERD (NBK554462, open access): GERD causes 25–55% of non-cardiac chest pain. Supporting: PMC3959479 (non-cardiac chest pain review). Replaced paywalled ACG 2022 guideline 2026-04-21; LR+ value unchanged.
NSAID use raises prior for peptic ulcer / gastritis causing chest pain. LR+ ~2.0 approximation.
Obesity (BMI>30) increases GERD risk. LR+ ~1.8 approximation (ACG 2022 cites obesity as risk factor; no single-feature LR pooled).
Tobacco use is a GERD risk factor (weak). LR+ ~1.5 approximation. Note: also a cardiac risk factor — use cautiously.
Absence of exertional trigger favours non-cardiac (GI/MSK). LR+ ~1.5 approximation for GI branch.
Chronic months-long symptom course favours GERD over acute cardiac/PE. LR+ ~2.0 approximation.

AAFP 2013: single-question screen for anxiety attack in past 4 weeks LR+ 4.2 / LR- 0.09 for panic disorder. Panic disorder is conventionally listed under non-cardiac chest pain diagnoses; sits on the 'GI/functional' branch as a catch-all non-cardiac non-pulmonary non-MSK category OR kept as a separate mini-branch. Mapped to gi here as the functional/visceral branch catch-all; revisit if branches.json subdivides.
